messages.content: Hey everyone! 😊 I was just thinking about some funny stuff from when I was a kid. Anyone else have any wild or embarrassing childhood stories they wanna share?

Omg yes, I have so many 😂 One time I tried to “run away” from home because my mom wouldn’t let me have a second pudding cup. I packed a backpack with like... socks and a banana, made it three houses down, and then got scared and came back.

Hahaha Charlie, that’s adorable 😂 I always love looking through old photo albums for those kinds of memories. My family has sooo many embarrassing pics of me with weird outfits or wild hair. Sometimes I think photos capture those moments better than my actual memory does!

Totally agree, Alice! Photos are the best for that. I found this one where I’m wearing a superhero cape made out of a towel and rain boots on the wrong feet 😂 No idea what my “mission” was but I looked very determined. Do any of you have a favorite old photo or family story?

Oh man, I love those kinds of photos! I have one where I’m grinning with a mouthful of missing teeth, wearing a princess dress over my pajamas and holding a plastic sword 😂 Apparently I was a “warrior queen” that day. My mom still pulls that one out to embarrass me at family gatherings. Anyone else have parents who LOVE showing off the most awkward pics?

Oh my gosh, yes! My mom has a whole collection labeled “Alice’s Best Looks” and it’s basically just a hall of fame for my worst fashion choices 😂 There’s one with me in a giant sunhat, raincoat, and cowboy boots—I thought I was starting a new trend. She still threatens to show it to anyone who visits!

This is bringing back so many memories 😂 My dad has this old photo of me at my 6th birthday party, face absolutely covered in cake and icing, and he insists on showing it to every new friend I bring home. I swear, parents live for those moments! Anyone else used to do something weird as a kid that your family still teases you about?

Oh, for sure! My family never lets me forget about the phase where I was obsessed with taping spoons to my hands and pretending I was a robot 😂 Apparently I wore them everywhere—school, grocery store, you name it. There’s photographic evidence too, unfortunately! I kind of wish I still had that level of imagination, honestly.

Haha Diana, I love the spoon hands! Kids just have the weirdest ideas and go all in. I used to pretend I was a detective and would “investigate” my parents by following them around the house with a notepad and sunglasses. I’m sure I was super subtle…not. Kind of miss being that confident in my weirdness 😄

Same, Charlie! I feel like we all had a secret talent for coming up with the most random games 😂 I used to pretend our living room floor was lava and made everyone hop from couch to chair. I took it SO seriously—my poor parents just wanted to sit down and relax but nope, the lava monster would get them! Wish adult life had more of that pure silliness sometimes.

Haha, the floor is lava was a classic! 😆 I remember turning the whole house into an “obstacle course” with pillows everywhere and making up ridiculous rules. My poor dog got roped in almost every time—he had no idea what was happening but he was a good sport. It really does make me want to bring back more silliness, even if it’s just for a day! Anyone else’s pets get caught up in your imaginary games?

Oh definitely! My childhood dog, Max, was basically my sidekick for every adventure 😂 He got dressed up as a pirate, a spy, even a “space dog” with a tinfoil helmet. He always looked a little confused but never tried to run off. Honestly, pets are the real MVPs for putting up with all our childhood schemes! Did anyone else ever make up wild stories about their pets’ secret lives?

Haha yes! I was convinced my cat had a double life as a secret agent. I’d make “spy missions” for her with yarn traps and “clues” hidden around the house. She was mostly interested in napping, but I was sure she was off saving the world when I wasn’t looking 😹 Looking back, I love how easy it was to imagine anything was possible as a kid.

I totally relate to that, Charlie! I used to write these super dramatic “diary entries” from my cat’s point of view about her secret adventures at night—like sneaking out to join a neighborhood animal club or solving mysteries 😂 Honestly, reading them now is hilarious. Childhood imagination was truly on another level! Anyone else find old drawings or stories and just crack up at how wild their ideas were?

Oh, absolutely! I found an old notebook full of “inventions” I designed as a kid—like a backpack with wings so you could fly to school, or shoes that made you bounce on the moon 😂 The drawings are completely ridiculous, but I was so convinced they’d work! It’s so funny (and kind of sweet) to see how our brains worked back then. Anyone else have a collection of hilarious childhood art or “inventions”?

Haha, yes! I found a whole folder of my old “comic books” last year. The stories made absolutely no sense—one was about a superhero potato who could turn invisible whenever someone tried to eat him 🥔💨 The drawings are so chaotic but I remember being really proud of them. It’s wild looking back and seeing how creative (and weird) our younger selves were! Anyone brave enough to share a pic of their best/worst childhood creation?

Omg, superhero potato is iconic 😂 I think I have a box somewhere with my old drawings—there’s definitely a unicorn that looks more like a chubby goat and a “time machine” that’s just a cardboard box with a bunch of random buttons glued on. I’ll try to dig them out and share a pic if I’m feeling brave enough! It’s actually kind of fun to look back and see how our little kid brains worked. Anyone else get nostalgic flipping through old stuff like that

Definitely! Going through old boxes is like opening a time capsule from a weirder, funnier universe. I always find some random craft or story I totally forgot about and end up laughing at how dramatic or inventive I was. Makes me want to keep some of the silly stuff we do now, just for future cringe/nostalgia purposes 😂 Anyone else have something random from childhood they just can’t throw out, no matter how silly it is?

Oh for sure—I still have this tiny stuffed bear I got when I was like three. He’s missing an eye and kind of falling apart, but I can’t bring myself to get rid of him. Every time I see him, it’s like instant flashbacks to building pillow forts and making him the “king” of the castle 😂 It’s funny how those little things hold so many memories. Anyone else have a totally beat-up item from when you were a kid that you just can’t let
Oh yes, I still have this weirdly lumpy clay “jewelry box” I made in second grade art class. The lid barely fits and it’s covered in glitter glue that’s turned

needle_message: Hahaha Charlie, that’s adorable 😂 I always love looking through old photo albums for those kinds of memories. My family has sooo many embarrassing pics of me with weird outfits or wild hair. Sometimes I think photos capture those moments better than my actual memory does!
